sanskrit: अमी हि त्वां सुरसङ्घा विशन्ति केचिद्भीताः प्राञ्जलयो गृणन्ति। स्वस्तीत्युक्त्वा महर्षिसिद्धसङ्घास्तुवन्ति त्वां स्तुतिभिः पुष्कलाभिः॥
transliteration: amī hi tvāṃ surasaṅghā viśanti kecid bhītāḥ prāñjalayo gṛṇanti | svastīty uktvā maharṣisiddhasaṅghāstuvanti tvāṃ stutibhiḥ puṣkalābhiḥ ||
literal_meaning: These hosts of gods are entering into You; some, in fear, with joined palms are calling out; uttering 'May all be well,' the companies of great seers and perfected ones praise You with ample hymns.
contextual_meaning: When the ego confronts the vast, uncontrollable power of the Self (Universal Form), every inner sub-personality—our inner critic, sage, achiever, lover—instinctively bows, some in awe, some in terror, all seeking safety through praise and surrender.
psychological_state: Peak transpersonal experience: ego boundaries thin, archetypal forces become visible, and the psyche simultaneously fears death and longs for merger.
trigger_scenario: Standing before a vast night sky, receiving an unexpected terminal diagnosis, or taking a high-dose psychedelic—moments when the self-image is dwarfed by immensity and every inner voice falls silent or prays.
anchor_text: Bow to the vast Self within; every inner voice finds peace in awe.
